title: Japan Defense Acquisition Agency Orders 300 Domestic Drones from Terra Drone
summary: Japan's Acquisition, Technology and Logistics Agency (ATLA) has contracted Terra Drone for 300 modular UAVs worth 115 million yen, marking a significant step in domestic drone procurement for defense purposes. Delivery remains scheduled for September 30, 2026.
contents:
## Japan Advances Domestic Drone Procurement for Defense

### Overview
Japan's defense acquisition arm, ATLA, finalized a 115.4 million yen ($780,000) manufacturing contract with Tokyo-based Terra Drone for 300 modular UAVs designated as educational platforms for the Japan Self-Defense Forces. The announcement, released on May 8, represents a concrete step in Japan's national policy of positioning unmanned systems at the core of its defense architecture.

### Strategic Context
Terra Drone entered the defense sector in March 2026, subsequently investing in Ukrainian counter-drone company Amazing Drones and deploying operational drones in Ukraine by April. The company characterized the contract as validation of Japan's domestic drone supply capability amid a global security environment undergoing fundamental transformation.

### Defense Industrial Implications
The procurement aligns with recommendations from ATLA's February report on strengthening Japan's defense industrial and technological base, which emphasized encouraging startups into defense through flexible contracts and maximizing commercial component usage in military drones. Prime Minister Takaichi's advisory panel on national security has specifically highlighted drone warfare lessons from Ukraine as a priority for Japan's revised defense strategy.

### Delivery and Next Steps
All 300 units carry a September 30 delivery deadline. The contract signals accelerating momentum in Japan's push to develop indigenous unmanned systems capability, moving beyond reliance on imported platforms.

title: Strait of Hormuz Shipping at Complete Standstill for 48 Hours Amid US-Iran Clashes
summary: No major commercial vessels have transited the Strait of Hormuz for 48 consecutive hours as US and Iranian naval forces exchange fire, with Iran attacking three US Navy destroyers and the US disabling Iranian-flagged tankers attempting to breach the blockade.
contents:
## Hormuz Shipping Crisis Deepens as Naval Clashes Escalate

### Current Situation
Ship tracking data compiled by Anadolu Agency confirms zero major commercial vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz for a consecutive 48-hour period as of 0900 GMT on May 9. The standstill follows renewed exchanges of fire between US and Iranian forces in and around the critical waterway.

### Naval Engagements
Iranian forces launched missiles, drones, and fast attack craft against USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115), and USS Truxton (DDG-103) as the destroyers transited toward the Gulf of Oman. US Central Command characterized the attacks as unprovoked and confirmed defensive responses. President Trump stated no damage occurred to the three destroyers while characterizing significant damage to Iranian attackers.

### Tanker Operations
US F/A-18 Super Hornets from USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) fired on two Iranian-flagged tankers, MT Sea Star III and MT Sevda, disabling them in the Gulf of Oman. Earlier in the week, another Super Hornet disabled tanker MT Hasna by firing 20mm cannon rounds into her rudder. Three cargo-empty tankers belonging to National Iranian Tanker Company managed to cross through the US Navy blockade line via Pakistan's Exclusive Economic Zone.

### Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority
Iran has formalized control over Hormuz transits through a newly created Persian Gulf Strait Authority, requiring all vessels to submit detailed declarations including cargo, crew nationalities, and destinations before receiving transit permission. The authority reportedly charges up to $2 million per vessel.

### Market Impact
US gasoline prices have risen above $4.50 per gallon. Lloyd's List tracked only 40 transits in the entire week ending May 3, compared to a pre-war average of 120 crossings per day.

title: Philippine Coast Guard Detects 40+ Chinese Vessels Including Research Ship Near Spratly Islands
summary: The Philippine Coast Guard spotted more than 40 Chinese vessels, including oceanographic research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33 and Chinese maritime militia ships, conducting unauthorized activities near the Spratly Islands within the Philippine exclusive economic zone.
contents:
## Philippines Challenges Chinese Maritime Presence in Spratly Waters

### Detection Summary
Philippine Coast Guard aircraft Islander 4177 detected Chinese oceanographic research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33 anchored approximately 7.34 nautical miles west of Rozul Reef during a maritime domain awareness flight. The vessel deployed a service boat heading toward the reef, confirming ongoing unauthorized marine scientific research operations.

### Vessel Disposition
The PCG identified Chinese Coast Guard vessel BN 5309 positioned at the western part of Rozul Reef alongside 13 Chinese maritime militia vessels anchored in surrounding waters. Near Pag-asa (Thitu) Island, an additional 28 Chinese maritime militia vessels clustered around Cay 1 and Cay 2, while China Coast Guard vessel 5101 maintained position approximately two nautical miles west of the island.

### Research Vessel Tracking
Canada's dark vessel detection system tracked Xiang Yang Hong 33 conducting survey activities across multiple Philippine EEZ features since departing China on April 15. The vessel operated near Ayungin Shoal (April 20-22), Escoda Shoal (April 22-28), Arellano Reef and Bulig Shoal (April 28 - May 1), Panganiban Reef (May 1-3), and Quirino Atoll and Rozul Reef (May 3-6).

### Philippine Response
PCG Commandant Admiral Ronnie Gil Gavan characterized the activities as clear violations of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), noting the Philippines has not granted consent for any Chinese marine scientific research in these waters. The AFP monitored 62 Chinese naval and coast guard vessels across four key West Philippine Sea features in April 2026.

title: Russia Holds Scaled-Back Victory Day Parade Featuring North Korean Troops for First Time
summary: Russia held its most modest Victory Day parade in history on Red Square, with no military equipment displayed and North Korean soldiers marching for the first time, as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire with Ukraine holds. Putin declared Russian troops fight for a just cause.
contents:
## Russia's Subdued Victory Day Amid Ukraine War and Ceasefire

### Parade Details
Russia conducted its 81st Victory Day military parade on Red Square on May 9, marking the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany. The event featured no tanks or other military equipment rolling over the cobblestones, making it the most scaled-back celebration in Russian Federation history. Soldiers and sailors, some having served in Ukraine, marched and cheered as Putin observed from beside Russian veterans near Lenin's Mausoleum.

### North Korean Participation
For the first time, troops from North Korea participated in the Victory Day parade, representing a tribute to Pyongyang's contribution of soldiers fighting alongside Russian forces to repel a Ukrainian incursion into Russia's Kursk region. The inclusion of North Korean military personnel underscored the deepening Moscow-Pyongyang defense relationship.

### Putin's Remarks
President Putin praised Russian troops in Ukraine, declaring they face an aggressive force supported by the entire NATO bloc and fight for a just cause. He proclaimed victory has always belonged to Russia and will continue to do so, emphasizing moral strength, courage, and national unity.

### Ceasefire Context
The parade occurred under a US-brokered three-day ceasefire running Saturday through Monday, supported by both the Kremlin and Kyiv. Both sides agreed to exchange 1,000 prisoners. Security remained tight in Moscow with air defense systems deployed, mobile communications restricted, and foreign media accreditation cancelled. At least 15 Russian regions declined to hold their own parades, citing drone threats.

### Broader Significance
The subdued celebration reflected growing war fatigue and economic strain. Jailed Russian nationalist Igor Girkin warned of a gradually deepening crisis, noting Russia's leaders appeared more concerned about losing their positions than about the broader trajectory of the conflict.

title: Satellite Imagery Reveals Philippine Infrastructure Expansion on Thitu and Nanshan Islands in Spratlys
summary: Planet Labs satellite imagery confirms active Philippine construction projects on Thitu Island's runway extension and Nanshan Island's port facility in the disputed Spratly Islands, funded by 1.95 billion pesos ($32 million) in Manila's 2025 budget allocations.
contents:
## Philippines Accelerates Spratly Islands Infrastructure Development

### Thitu Island Runway Extension
Satellite imagery captured by Planet Labs confirms a roughly 200-meter runway extension under construction at Thitu (Pag-asa) Island, the only Philippine-administered Spratly feature with a permanent civilian population. The expansion will lengthen the existing 1.3-kilometer runway to 1.5 kilometers, sufficient to accommodate F-16 variants and light combat aircraft. Manila allocated 1.65 billion pesos ($27 million) for this project in its 2025 budget.

### Construction Progress
April 2 imagery shows a barge positioned on the runway's western section, delivering construction materials. The extension occupies an area that previously remained submerged, measuring approximately 200 meters in length and 100 meters in width. Construction commenced shortly after budget approval and continues as of May 2026.

### Nanshan Island Port Development
A separate 300 million peso ($5 million) sheltered port project at Nanshan (Lawak) Island remains in early stages. Satellite imagery from October 2025 through April 2026 documents ongoing work to replace the current port, which has a narrow entry channel too shallow for larger vessels.

### Strategic Assessment
Harrison Pretat of the Asia Maritime Transparency Initiative identified tensions at Second Thomas Shoal as the primary driver for these upgrades, noting they enhance logistics capabilities and resilience of Philippine outposts. Analysts suggest the expanded runway could accommodate US military aircraft if cooperation deepens, potentially shifting strategic pressure on China in the contested waters.

title: Nearly 1,000 Vessels and 20,000 Seafarers Remain Stranded in Persian Gulf as Hormuz Crisis Continues
summary: The International Maritime Organization reports nearly 1,000 vessels carrying approximately 20,000 seafarers remain trapped in the Persian Gulf due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, with the 32nd maritime attack since the war began injuring eight crew members aboard CMA CGM San Antonio.
contents:
## Humanitarian Maritime Crisis Deepens in Persian Gulf

### Stranded Fleet
Approximately 20,000 seafarers aboard nearly 1,000 vessels remain trapped inside the Persian Gulf as the Strait of Hormuz standstill continues. Maritime risk consultancy Marisks CEO Dimitris Maniatis emphasized that mariners caught in the middle represent civilians managing global trade, not combatants, and should not face such conditions.

### Latest Attack Toll
The IMO confirmed eight crew members of CMA CGM San Antonio sustained wounds when the container ship came under attack while attempting a night transit near Oman. The incident, which marks the 32nd maritime attack since the US-Iran conflict commenced on February 28, involved an Iranian projectile striking the Maltese-flagged vessel. The injured Filipino crew members received evacuation for medical treatment.

### Shipping Industry Impact
Lloyd's List Editor-in-Chief Richard Meade reported a 40 percent decrease in strait transits from April 13-20, followed by further collapse to near-zero commercial traffic. The shipping industry received no prior consultation before Trump's Project Freedom announcement and faced confusion as the operation launched and suspended within 48 hours.

### Insurance and Commercial Fallout
Shipping association BIMCO warned that transits without Iranian coordination entail significant risk. Multiple shipowners told Bloomberg they would not contact Iran's new transit authority, wary of providing precise vessel movement details. War risk insurance premiums have escalated dramatically, adding to the economic burden on global trade.

title: South Korea Resumes Heavy Capital Gains Tax on Multi-Homeowners, Reviews Rental Landlord Benefits
summary: South Korea's temporary suspension of capital gains tax surcharges for multi-property owners expired on May 9, reimposing up to 82.5% tax rates. Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol announced a review of perpetual tax exemptions for purchase-for-lease apartment operators, potentially releasing 25,000 Seoul units to market.
contents:
## South Korea Real Estate Tax Policy Enters New Phase

### Tax Resumption
The four-month suspension of heavy capital gains tax surcharges for owners of multiple properties ended on May 9, 2026. Starting May 10, sellers of additional properties face up to 82.5% taxation (including local income tax) on capital gains. The government initially announced the suspension end on January 23 to encourage multi-homeowners to divest excess properties.

### Market Response
Deputy Prime Minister Koo Yun-cheol stated at an emergency economic headquarters meeting that the real estate market now undergoes reorganization centered on actual residents, moving away from past speculative overheating patterns. Among Seoul apartment listings in March, 2,087 units originated from multi-homeowners, up 32% year-over-year, while homeless first-time buyers comprised 73% of purchasers.

### Rental Landlord Review
The government signaled a review of capital gains tax benefits for purchase-for-lease apartment operators, characterizing their permanent exemption from heavy taxation as excessive from a tax equity perspective. If included in the second-half tax overhaul, approximately 25,000 Seoul apartment units with expired mandatory lease periods could enter the market.

### Economic Context
Koo noted South Korea's March current account surplus reached a record $37.3 billion and exports exceeded $80 billion for the second consecutive month, demonstrating solid economic fundamentals despite Middle East war pressures. The government extended petroleum price caps for a fifth time, freezing gasoline at 1,934 won per liter through May 22.

title: China Confirms Attack on Chinese-Owned Oil Tanker Near Strait of Hormuz, Expresses Deep Concern
summary: China's Foreign Ministry confirmed an oil products tanker carrying Chinese crew sustained an attack near the Strait of Hormuz, with no reported crew casualties. The vessel, believed to have carried a CHINA OWNER AND CREW marking, reportedly suffered a deck fire near the UAE coast.
contents:
## China Acknowledges Tanker Attack Amid Hormuz Maritime Crisis

### Official Confirmation
Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian confirmed during a regular press conference on May 8 that an oil products tanker carrying Chinese crew sustained an attack near the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson expressed deep concern about vessels affected by the ongoing Middle East conflict while noting no crew casualties occurred.

### Vessel Identification
Chinese media outlet Caixin reported a Chinese-owned tanker marked with prominent signage reading CHINA OWNER AND CREW came under attack on Monday, May 5. Maritime security sources identified the damaged vessel as the Marshall Islands-flagged oil products and chemical tanker JV Innovation, which reported a deck fire to nearby ships. The incident occurred off the UAE coast near Mina Saqr.

### Diplomatic Dimensions
The attack preceded a meeting between China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi in Beijing on Wednesday, where they discussed reopening the strait. China has maintained its position as a major buyer of Iranian oil since the conflict began, with March imports from Iran largely unaffected.

### Broader Context
The confirmation comes as the US-Iran conflict has stranded hundreds of vessels and approximately 20,000 seafarers inside the Gulf. Traffic through the strait has suffered severe disruption from renewed attacks this week, with the US and Iran exchanging fire again on Thursday while Washington awaited Tehran's response to a proposed ceasefire extension.

title: China Emphasizes Taiwan as Core Priority Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit, Wang Yi Meets US Senators
summary: China's Foreign Ministry declared the Taiwan question constitutes the bedrock of China-US relations ahead of the May 14-15 Trump-Xi summit, while Foreign Minister Wang Yi told visiting US senators led by Steve Daines that bilateral ties have maintained overall stability despite disruptions.
contents:
## China Intensifies Taiwan Messaging Before Presidential Summit

### Diplomatic Signaling
Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian declared on May 8 that the Taiwan question represents the core of China's core interests and the bedrock of the political foundation of China-US relations. The statement demands US adherence to the one-China principle and the three joint communiques as prerequisites for a steady, sound, and sustainable relationship. Defense expert Arthur Zhin-Sheng Wang of Taiwan's Central Police University characterized such strong language so close to a summit as rare for Beijing.

### Congressional Engagement
Foreign Minister Wang Yi hosted a bipartisan US congressional delegation led by Senator Steve Daines, crediting Presidents Xi Jinping and Trump for steering bilateral relations at critical moments. Daines emphasized the desire to de-escalate rather than decouple, seeking stability and mutual respect. He hinted at potential Boeing aircraft purchases as a summit deliverable and recognized China's Middle East engagement, specifically Wang's meeting with Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi.

### Summit Preparations
The May 14-15 Trump visit to China follows weeks of heightened diplomatic activity. China and the US remain in communication on summit logistics. Washington has pressed Beijing to leverage its influence with Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz reopening. Wang Yi previously told Secretary Rubio that the Taiwan question constitutes the biggest risk in bilateral relations.

### Military Pressure
Beijing continues near-daily deployment of warplanes and naval vessels around Taiwan, maintaining military pressure while simultaneously pursuing diplomatic engagement with Washington. The language explicitly linking US diplomatic actions on Taiwan to the broader relationship represents an escalation in rhetorical framing.

title: Japan SDF Completes Largest-Ever Balikatan Exercise Participation with 1,400 Personnel and Historic Missile Firing
summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces completed their largest-ever participation in the US-Philippines Balikatan exercise with approximately 1,400 personnel, including the first overseas firing of Type 88 surface-to-ship guided missiles from Paoay, northern Philippines.
contents:
## Japan's Historic Military Exercise Milestone in the Philippines

### Unprecedented Scale
The Japan Self-Defense Forces deployed approximately 1,400 personnel to the 2026 Balikatan exercise, nearly ten times the 150 sent in 2025. The massive increase became feasible through the Japan-Philippines Reciprocal Access Agreement (RAA) that took effect in 2025, simplifying visa procedures and weapons transfer protocols for joint training.

### Historic Missile Launch
During live-fire drills at Paoay on the island of Luzon, Ground Self-Defense Force units fired two Type 88 surface-to-ship guided missiles at a target approximately 75 kilometers offshore, marking the first time Japan has launched offensive missiles from foreign soil. Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi personally observed the exercise. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian condemned the firing, citing Japan's history of militarist aggression and exclusively defense-oriented policy.

### Exercise Context
The Balikatan 2026 exercise ran from April 20 through May 8, involving more than 17,000 troops from seven nations including the US, Philippines, Japan, Australia, France, Canada, and New Zealand. The exercises featured amphibious operations, missile defense drills, and counter-landing live fire demonstrations conducted near the strategically significant Bashi Channel between Taiwan and the Philippines.

### Strategic Implications
Senior Japanese Defense Ministry officials characterized the exercises as effective in checking China's military assertiveness. Former GSDF Chief of Staff Yoshifumi Hibako emphasized that mutual understanding of equipment and command structures can only emerge through joint exercises. Japan simultaneously agreed with the Philippines to establish a working group on transferring Abukuma-class destroyer escorts, potentially Japan's first lethal weapons export under revised transfer principles.
